message.content:
Explore the Go directory `internal/framework/` in the nginx-gateway-fabric project (working dir is the repo root). Do a "very thorough" sweep. This framework appears to be a reusable in-house framework. I need a structured map of every subpackage and its responsibility, plus the key files and types/functions.

For EACH subpackage under internal/framework/, report: purpose (1-2 sentences), the most important 1-5 source files (full relative paths), and the key types/interfaces/functions defined there. Cover at least: helpers, controller, events, status, sdk, register, runnables, context, convert, dataplane, conditions, parallel, sort, static, target, and any others.

Focus especially on:
- How the framework abstracts controller-runtime (look for manager/controller builder patterns).
- Any "processor" or pipeline abstraction.
- The events / status / conditions machinery.
- Interfaces that the controller layer implements (dataplane, status, etc).

Return a concise structured text report. Do NOT dump entire file contents — give me conclusions, file paths, and the names of key symbols/interfaces.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

20	// NamespacedNameFilterFunc is a function that returns true if the resource should be processed by the reconciler.
21	// If the function returns false, the reconciler will log the returned string.
22	type NamespacedNameFilterFunc func(nsname types.NamespacedName) (shouldProcess bool, msg string)
23	
24	// ReconcilerConfig is the configuration for the reconciler.
25	type ReconcilerConfig struct {
26		// Getter gets a resource from the k8s API.
27		Getter Getter
28		// ObjectType is the type of the resource that the reconciler will reconcile.
29		ObjectType ngftypes.ObjectType
30		// EventCh is the channel where the reconciler will send events.
31		EventCh chan<- any
32		// NamespacedNameFilter filters resources the controller will process. Can be nil.
33		NamespacedNameFilter NamespacedNameFilterFunc
34		// OnlyMetadata indicates that this controller for this resource is only caching metadata for the resource.
35		OnlyMetadata bool
36	}
37	
38	// Reconciler reconciles Kubernetes resources of a specific type.
39	// It implements the reconcile.Reconciler interface.
40	// A successful reconciliation of a resource has the two possible outcomes:
41	// (1) If the resource is deleted, the Implementation will send a DeleteEvent to the event channel.
42	// (2) If the resource is upserted (created or updated), the Implementation will send an UpsertEvent
43	// to the event channel.
44	type Reconciler struct {
45		cfg ReconcilerConfig
46	}
47	
48	var _ reconcile.Reconciler = &Reconciler{}
49	
50	// NewReconciler creates a new reconciler.
51	func NewReconciler(cfg ReconcilerConfig) *Reconciler {
52		return &Reconciler{
53			cfg: cfg,
54		}
55	}
56	
57	func (r *Reconciler) mustCreateNewObject(objectType ngftypes.ObjectType) ngftypes.ObjectType {
58		if r.cfg.OnlyMetadata {
59			partialObj := &metav1.PartialObjectMetadata{}
60			partialObj.SetGroupVersionKind(objectType.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind())
61	
62			return partialObj
63		}
64	
65		// without Elem(), t will be a pointer to the type. For example, *v1.Gateway, not v1.Gateway
66		t := reflect.TypeOf(objectType).Elem()
67	
68		// We could've used objectType.DeepCopyObject() here, but it's a bit slower confirmed by benchmarks.
69		obj, ok := reflect.New(t).Interface().(client.Object)
70		if !ok {
71			panic("failed to create a new object")
72		}
73	
74		// reflect.New creates a zero-valued instance, which loses the GVK for unstructured objects
75		// since they store GVK at runtime rather than in their struct definition.
76		if _, isUnstructured := obj.(*unstructured.Unstructured); isUnstructured {
77			obj.GetObjectKind().SetGroupVersionKind(objectType.GetObjectKind().GroupVersionKind())
78		}
79	
80		return obj
81	}
82	
83	// Reconcile implements the reconcile.Reconciler Reconcile method.
84	func (r *Reconciler) Reconcile(ctx context.Context, req reconcile.Request) (reconcile.Result, error) {
85		logger := log.FromContext(ctx)
86		// The controller runtime has set the logger with the group, kind, namespace and name of the resource,
87		// and a few other key/value pairs. So we don't need to set them here.
88	
89		logger.Info("Reconciling the resource")
90	
91		if r.cfg.NamespacedNameFilter != nil {
92			if shouldProcess, msg := r.cfg.NamespacedNameFilter(req.NamespacedName); !shouldProcess {
93				logger.Info(msg)
94				return reconcile.Result{}, nil
95			}
96		}
97	
98		obj := r.mustCreateNewObject(r.cfg.ObjectType)
99	
100		if err := r.cfg.Getter.Get(ctx, req.NamespacedName, obj); err != nil {
101			if !apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
102				logger.Error(err, "Failed to get the resource")
103				return reconcile.Result{}, err
104			}
105			// The resource does not exist (was deleted).
106			obj = nil
107		}
108	
109		var e any
110		var op string
111	
112		if obj == nil {
113			e = &events.DeleteEvent{
114				Type:           r.cfg.ObjectType,
115				NamespacedName: req.NamespacedName,
116			}
117			op = "Deleted"
118		} else {
119			e = &events.UpsertEvent{
120				Resource: obj,
121			}
122			op = "Upserted"
123		}
124	
125		select {
126		case <-ctx.Done():
127			logger.Info("Did not process the resource because the context was canceled")
128			return reconcile.Result{}, nil
129		case r.cfg.EventCh <- e:
130		}
131	
132		logger.Info(fmt.Sprintf("%s the resource", op))
133	
134		return reconcile.Result{}, nil
135	}

3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"

7		"github.com/go-logr/logr"
8	)

10	// EventLoop is the main event loop of the Gateway. It handles events coming through the event channel.
11	//
12	// When a new event comes, there are two cases:
13	// - If there is no event(s) currently being handled, the new event is handled immediately.
14	// - Otherwise, the new event will be saved for later handling. All saved events will be handled after the handling of
15	// the current event(s) finishes. Multiple saved events will be handled at once -- they will be batched.
16	//
17	// Batching is needed because handling an event (or multiple events at once) will typically result in
18	// reloading NGINX, which is an operation we want to minimize for the following reasons:
19	// (1) A reload takes time - at least 200ms. The time depends on the size of the configuration, the number of
20	// TLS certs, and the number of available CPU cycles.
21	// (2) A reload can have side-effects for data plane traffic.
22	// FIXME(pleshakov): better document the side effects and how to prevent and mitigate them.
23	// So when the EventLoop have 100 saved events, it is better to process them at once rather than one by one.
24	// https://github.com/nginx/nginx-gateway-fabric/issues/551
25	type EventLoop struct {
26		handler  EventHandler
27		preparer FirstEventBatchPreparer
28		eventCh  <-chan any
29		logger   logr.Logger
30	
31		// The EventLoop uses double buffering to handle event batch processing.
32		// The goroutine that handles the batch will always read from the currentBatch slice.
33		// While the current batch is being handled, new events are added to the nextBatch slice.
34		// The batches are swapped before starting the handler goroutine.
35		currentBatch EventBatch
36		nextBatch    EventBatch
37	
38		// the ID of the current batch
39		currentBatchID int
40	}
41	
42	// NewEventLoop creates a new EventLoop.
43	func NewEventLoop(
44		eventCh <-chan any,
45		logger logr.Logger,
46		handler EventHandler,
47		preparer FirstEventBatchPreparer,
48	) *EventLoop {
49		return &EventLoop{
50			eventCh:      eventCh,
51			logger:       logger,
52			handler:      handler,
53			preparer:     preparer,
54			currentBatch: make(EventBatch, 0),
55			nextBatch:    make(EventBatch, 0),
56		}
57	}
58	
59	// Start starts the EventLoop.
60	// This method will block until the EventLoop stops, which will happen after the ctx is closed.
61	func (el *EventLoop) Start(ctx context.Context) error {
62		// handling tells if any batch is currently being handled.
63		var handling bool
64		// handlingDone is used to signal the completion of handling a batch.
65		handlingDone := make(chan struct{})
66	
67		handleBatch := func() {
68			go func(batch EventBatch) {
69				el.currentBatchID++
70				batchLogger := el.logger.WithName("eventHandler").WithValues("batchID", el.currentBatchID)
71	
72				batchLogger.V(1).Info("Handling events from the batch", "total", len(batch))
73	
74				el.handler.HandleEventBatch(ctx, batchLogger, batch)
75	
76				batchLogger.V(1).Info("Finished handling the batch")
77				handlingDone <- struct{}{}
78			}(el.currentBatch)
79		}
80	
81		swapAndHandleBatch := func() {
82			el.swapBatches()
83			handleBatch()
84			handling = true
85		}
86	
87		// Prepare the fist event batch, which includes the UpsertEvents for all relevant cluster resources.
88		// This is necessary so that the first time the EventHandler generates NGINX configuration, it derives it from
89		// a complete view of the cluster. Otherwise, the handler would generate incomplete configuration, which can lead
90		// to clients seeing transient 404 errors from NGINX and incorrect statuses of the resources updated by the Gateway.
91		//
92		// Note:
93		// After the handler goroutine handles the first batch, the loop will start receiving events from
94		// the controllers, which at the beginning will be UpsertEvents with the relevant cluster resources - i.e. they
95		// will be duplicates of the events in the first batch. This is OK, because it is expected that the EventHandler will
96		// not trigger any reconfiguration after receiving an upsert for an existing resource with the same Generation.
97	
98		var err error
99		el.currentBatch, err = el.preparer.Prepare(ctx)
100		if err != nil {
101			return fmt.Errorf("failed to prepare the first batch: %w", err)
102		}
103	
104		// Handle the first batch
105		handleBatch()
106		handling = true
107	
108		// Note: at any point of time, no more than one batch is currently being handled.
109	
110		// The event loop
111		for {
112			select {
113			case <-ctx.Done():
114				// Wait for the completion if a batch is being handled.
115				if handling {
116					<-handlingDone
117				}
118				return nil
119			case e := <-el.eventCh:
120				// Add the event to the current batch.
121				el.nextBatch = append(el.nextBatch, e)
122	
123				el.logger.V(1).Info(
124					"added an event to the next batch",
125					"type", fmt.Sprintf("%T", e),
126					"total", len(el.nextBatch),
127				)
128	
129				// If no batch is currently being handled, swap batches and begin handling the batch.
130				if !handling {
131					swapAndHandleBatch()
132				}
133			case <-handlingDone:
134				handling = false
135	
136				// If there's at least one event in the next batch, swap batches and begin handling the batch.
137				if len(el.nextBatch) > 0 {
138					swapAndHandleBatch()
139				}
140			}
141		}
142	}
143	
144	// swapBatches swaps the current and next batches.
145	func (el *EventLoop) swapBatches() {
146		el.currentBatch, el.nextBatch = el.nextBatch, el.currentBatch
147		el.nextBatch = el.nextBatch[:0]
148	}

15	
16	//counterfeiter:generate . FirstEventBatchPreparer
17	
18	// FirstEventBatchPreparer prepares the first batch of events to be processed by the EventHandler.
19	// The first batch includes the UpsertEvents for all relevant resources in the cluster.
20	type FirstEventBatchPreparer interface {
21		// Prepare prepares the first event batch.
22		Prepare(ctx context.Context) (EventBatch, error)
23	}
24	
25	// EachListItemFunc lists each item of a client.ObjectList.
26	// It is from k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/meta.
27	type EachListItemFunc func(obj runtime.Object, fn func(runtime.Object) error) error
28	
29	// FirstEventBatchPreparerImpl is an implementation of FirstEventBatchPreparer.
30	type FirstEventBatchPreparerImpl struct {
31		reader       kubernetes.Reader
32		eachListItem EachListItemFunc
33		objects      []client.Object
34		objectLists  []client.ObjectList
35	}
36	
37	// NewFirstEventBatchPreparerImpl creates a new FirstEventBatchPreparerImpl.
38	// objects and objectList specify which resources will be included in the first batch.
39	// For each object from objects, FirstEventBatchPreparerImpl will get the corresponding resource from the reader.
40	// The object must specify its namespace (if any) and name.
41	// For each list from objectLists, FirstEventBatchPreparerImpl will list the resources of the corresponding type from
42	// the reader.
43	func NewFirstEventBatchPreparerImpl(
44		reader kubernetes.Reader,
45		objects []client.Object,
46		objectLists []client.ObjectList,
47	) *FirstEventBatchPreparerImpl {
48		return &FirstEventBatchPreparerImpl{
49			reader:       reader,
50			objects:      objects,
51			objectLists:  objectLists,
52			eachListItem: meta.EachListItem,
53		}
54	}
55	
56	// SetEachListItem sets the EachListItemFunc function.
57	// Used for unit testing.
58	func (p *FirstEventBatchPreparerImpl) SetEachListItem(eachListItem EachListItemFunc) {
59		p.eachListItem = eachListItem
60	}
61	
62	func (p *FirstEventBatchPreparerImpl) Prepare(ctx context.Context) (EventBatch, error) {
63		total := 0
64	
65		for _, list := range p.objectLists {
66			if err := p.reader.List(ctx, list); err != nil {
67				return nil, err
68			}
69	
70			total += meta.LenList(list)
71		}
72	
73		// If some of p.objects don't exist, they will not be added to the batch. In that case, the capacity will be greater
74		// than the length, but it is OK, because len(p.objects) is small.
75		batch := make([]any, 0, total+len(p.objects))
76	
77		for _, obj := range p.objects {
78			key := types.NamespacedName{Namespace: obj.GetNamespace(), Name: obj.GetName()}
79	
80			if err := p.reader.Get(ctx, key, obj); err != nil {
81				if !apierrors.IsNotFound(err) {
82					return nil, err
83				}
84			} else {
85				batch = append(batch, &UpsertEvent{Resource: obj})
86			}
87		}
88	
89		// Note: the order of the events doesn't matter.
90	
91		for _, list := range p.objectLists {
92			err := p.eachListItem(list, func(object runtime.Object) error {
93				clientObj, ok := object.(client.Object)
94				if !ok {
95					return fmt.Errorf("cannot cast %T to client.Object", object)
96				}
97				batch = append(batch, &UpsertEvent{Resource: clientObj})
98				return nil
99			})
100			if err != nil {
101				return nil, err
102			}
103		}
104	
105		return batch, nil
106	}

19		// secretFileMode defines the default file mode for files with secrets as an integer.

28	func (t Type) String() string {
29		switch t {
30		case TypeRegular:
31			return "Regular"
32		case TypeSecret:

34		default:

41		TypeRegular Type = iota
42		// TypeSecret is the type for secret files.
43		TypeSecret

47	type File struct {

53	//counterfeiter:generate . OSFileManager
54	
55	// OSFileManager is an interface that exposes File I/O operations.
56	type OSFileManager interface {
57		// Create file at the provided filepath.
58		Create(name string) (*os.File, error)
59		// Chmod sets the mode of the file.
60		Chmod(file *os.File, mode os.FileMode) error
61		// Write writes contents to the file.
62		Write(file *os.File, contents []byte) error
63		// Open opens the file.
64		Open(name string) (*os.File, error)
65		// Copy copies from src to dst.
66		Copy(dst io.Writer, src io.Reader) error
67	}
68	
69	func Write(fileMgr OSFileManager, file File) error {
70		ensureType(file.Type)
71	
72		f, err := fileMgr.Create(file.Path)
73		if err != nil {
74			return fmt.Errorf("failed to create file %q: %w", file.Path, err)
75		}
76	
77		var resultErr error
78	
79		defer func() {
80			if err := f.Close(); err != nil {
81				resultErr = errors.Join(resultErr, fmt.Errorf("failed to close file %q: %w", file.Path, err))
82			}
83		}()
84	
85		switch file.Type {
86		case TypeRegular:
87			if err := fileMgr.Chmod(f, RegularFileModeInt); err != nil {
88				resultErr = fmt.Errorf(
89					"failed to set file mode to %#o for %q: %w", RegularFileModeInt, file.Path, err)
90				return resultErr
91			}
92		case TypeSecret:
93			if err := fileMgr.Chmod(f, secretFileModeInt); err != nil {
94				resultErr = fmt.Errorf("failed to set file mode to %#o for %q: %w", secretFileModeInt, file.Path, err)
95				return resultErr
96			}
97		default:
98			panic(fmt.Sprintf("unknown file type %d", file.Type))
99		}
100	
101		if err := fileMgr.Write(f, file.Content); err != nil {
102			resultErr = fmt.Errorf("failed to write file %q: %w", file.Path, err)
103			return resultErr
104		}
105	
106		return resultErr
107	}
108	
109	func ensureType(fileType Type) {
110		if fileType != TypeRegular && fileType != TypeSecret {
111			panic(fmt.Sprintf("unknown file type %d", fileType))
112		}
113	}
114	
115	// Convert an agent File to an internal File type.
116	func Convert(agentFile agent.File) File {
117		if agentFile.Meta == nil {
118			return File{}
119		}
120	
121		var t Type
122		switch agentFile.Meta.Permissions {
123		case RegularFileMode:
124			t = TypeRegular
125		case SecretFileMode:
126			t = TypeSecret
127		}
128	
129		return File{
130			Content: agentFile.Contents,
131			Path:    agentFile.Meta.Name,
132			Type:    t,
133		}
134	}

1	package controller

3	import (
4		"crypto/sha256"
5		"encoding/hex"
6		"strings"
7	)
8	
9	const (
10		// inferencePoolServiceSuffix is the suffix of the headless Service name for an InferencePool.
11		inferencePoolServiceSuffix = "pool-svc"
12		MaxServiceNameLen          = 63
13		hashLen                    = 8
14	)
15	
16	// CreateNginxResourceName creates the base resource name for all nginx resources
17	// created by the control plane.
18	func CreateNginxResourceName(prefix, suffix string) string {
19		return truncateAndHashName(prefix, suffix)
20	}
21	
22	// CreateInferencePoolServiceName creates the name for a headless Service that
23	// we create for an InferencePool.
24	func CreateInferencePoolServiceName(name string) string {
25		return truncateAndHashName(name, inferencePoolServiceSuffix)
26	}
27	
28	// truncateAndHashName truncates the input name to fit within maxLen,
29	// appending a hash for uniqueness if needed.
30	func truncateAndHashName(name string, suffix string) string {
31		sep := "-"
32		full := name + sep + suffix
33		if len(full) <= MaxServiceNameLen {
34			return full
35		}
36	
37		// Always include the suffix, truncate name as needed
38		hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(full))
39		hashStr := hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])[:hashLen]
40		maxNameLen := MaxServiceNameLen - (len(sep) * 2) - hashLen - len(suffix)
41		truncName := name
42		if len(name) > maxNameLen {
43			truncName = name[:maxNameLen]
44		}
45	
46		// Remove trailing dashes to avoid double-dash when separator is added
47		truncName = strings.TrimRight(truncName, sep)
48	
49		return truncName + sep + hashStr + sep + suffix
50	}

168		// For HTTP sources: GETs req.URL; sends If-None-Match when req.ETag is set, or If-Modified-Since
169		// when req.LastModified is set;

215	// validateAndNormalizeRequest checks mutual-exclusion rules and normalises
216	// ExpectedChecksum to lowercase. It returns the updated Request or an error.
217	func validateAndNormalizeRequest(req Request) (Request, error) {
218		if req.VerifyChecksum &&
219			(req.N1C.Namespace != "" || req.PolicyName != "" || req.NIM.PolicyUID != "" || req.LogProfileName != "") {
220			return Request{}, fmt.Errorf(
221				"verifyChecksum is only supported for plain HTTP fetches; use expectedChecksum for NIM/N1C sources",
222			)
223		}
224	
225		if req.ExpectedChecksum != "" {
226			normalized := strings.ToLower(req.ExpectedChecksum)
227			if _, err := hex.DecodeString(normalized); err != nil || len(normalized) != 64 {
228				return Request{}, fmt.Errorf(
229					"invalid expected checksum %q: must be 64 hex characters", req.ExpectedChecksum,
230				)
231			}
232			req.ExpectedChecksum = normalized
233		}
234	
235		return req, nil
236	}

247	// When req.N1C.Namespace is set, uses N1C fetch logic (APIToken auth, N1C API path).
248	// When req.PolicyName or req.NIM.PolicyUID is set (and N1C.Namespace is empty), uses NIM fetch logic.
249	// Otherwise performs a plain GET to req.URL, optionally verifying the checksum.
250	// For plain HTTP sources, a conditional GET is issued when req.ETag or req.LastModified is set.

267		result, err := f.fetch(ctx, req, f.dispatchChecksum)
268		return result.Checksum, err

272	// and N1C sources without downloading the full bundle content. Returns an error for plain HTTP sources.

278	func (f *HTTPFetcher) dispatchChecksum(
279		ctx context.Context,
280		client *http.Client,
281		req Request,
282	) (Result, error) {
283		switch {
284		case req.N1C.Namespace != "":
285			checksum, err := fetchN1CChecksum(ctx, client, req, f.n1cCompilePollDelay, f.logger)
286			return Result{Checksum: checksum}, err
287		case req.PolicyName != "" || req.NIM.PolicyUID != "":
288			checksum, err := fetchNIMChecksum(ctx, client, req, f.logger)
289			return Result{Checksum: checksum}, err
290		default:
291			return Result{}, &nonTransientError{
292				err: fmt.Errorf("FetchPolicyBundleChecksum is not supported for plain HTTP sources"),
293			}
294		}
295	}
296	
297	func (f *HTTPFetcher) logProfileDispatchChecksum(
298		ctx context.Context,
299		client *http.Client,
300		req Request,
301	) (Result, error) {
302		switch {
303		case req.N1C.Namespace != "":
304			checksum, err := fetchN1CLogProfileChecksum(ctx, client, req, f.n1cCompilePollDelay, f.logger)
305			return Result{Checksum: checksum}, err
306		case req.LogProfileName != "":
307			checksum, err := fetchNIMLogProfileChecksum(ctx, client, req)
308			return Result{Checksum: checksum}, err
309		default:
310			return Result{}, &nonTransientError{
311				err: fmt.Errorf("FetchLogProfileBundleChecksum is not supported for plain HTTP sources"),
312			}
313		}
314	}
315	
316	func (f *HTTPFetcher) fetch(
317		ctx context.Context,
318		req Request,
319		dispatch func(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, req Request) (Result, error),
320	) (Result, error) {
321		var err error
322		if req, err = validateAndNormalizeRequest(req); err != nil {
323			return Result{}, err
324		}
325	
326		client, err := f.buildHTTPClient(req)
327		if err != nil {
328			return Result{}, err
329		}
330		// buildHTTPClient creates a custom transport when TLS or insecure settings are configured.
331		// Unlike the shared default transport, this transport is not managed globally, so we close
332		// its idle connections when Fetch returns to prevent connection leaks.
333		if len(req.TLSCAData) > 0 || req.InsecureSkipVerify {
334			defer client.CloseIdleConnections()
335		}
336	
337		// FIXME(ciarams87): The retry loop runs synchronously
338		// inside Process(), which blocks the event handler and delays all config pushes to NGINX until
339		// retries complete. We should investigate making this async as retry
340		// counts or server latency could grow large enough to cause meaningful delays.
341		backoff := newRetryBackoff(req.RetryAttempts)
342	
343		var (
344			result  Result
345			lastErr error
346		)
347		attempt := 0
348		err = wait.ExponentialBackoffWithContext(ctx, backoff, func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error) {
349			attempt++
350			fr, fetchErr := dispatch(ctx, client, req)
351			if fetchErr != nil {
352				var nte *nonTransientError
353				if errors.As(fetchErr, &nte) {
354					return false, fetchErr
355				}
356				lastErr = fetchErr
357				f.logger.V(1).Info("Transient fetch error, retrying",
358					"attempt", attempt, "maxAttempts", backoff.Steps, "error", fetchErr)
359				return false, nil
360			}
361			result = fr
362			return true, nil
363		})
364		if err != nil {
365			if wait.Interrupted(err) {
366				return Result{}, lastErr
367			}
368			return Result{}, err
369		}
370	
371		if !result.Unchanged && req.ExpectedChecksum != "" && result.Checksum != req.ExpectedChecksum {
372			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf(
373				"bundle checksum mismatch: expected %s, got %s", req.ExpectedChecksum, result.Checksum,
374			)
375		}
376	
377		return result, nil
378	}

406	// newRetryBackoff returns a Backoff configured with the project-wide retry defaults.
407	// Steps is set to retryAttempts+1 so that the initial attempt is always made even
408	// when retryAttempts is zero.
409	func newRetryBackoff(retryAttempts int32) wait.Backoff {
410		return wait.Backoff{
411			Duration: retryBaseDelay,
412			Factor:   2.0,
413			Jitter:   1.0,
414			Cap:      retryMaxDelay,
415			Steps:    int(retryAttempts) + 1,
416		}

419	// buildHTTPClient returns an *http.Client configured for the given request.
420	func (f *HTTPFetcher) buildHTTPClient(req Request) (*http.Client, error) {
421		timeout := defaultTimeout
422		if req.Timeout != nil {
423			timeout = req.Timeout.Duration
424		}
425	
426		if len(req.TLSCAData) == 0 && !req.InsecureSkipVerify {
427			c := *f.defaultClient
428			c.Timeout = timeout
429			return &c, nil
430		}
431	
432		client, err := buildClient(req.TLSCAData, req.InsecureSkipVerify, timeout)
433		if err != nil {
434			return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to build HTTP client: %w", err)
435		}
436		return client, nil
437	}

439	// buildClient returns an *http.Client with the given timeout. When caData is non-nil, the CA is
440	// appended to the system cert pool. When insecureSkipVerify is true, TLS verification is disabled.
441	func buildClient(caData []byte, insecureSkipVerify bool, timeout time.Duration) (*http.Client, error) {
442		if caData == nil && !insecureSkipVerify {
443			return &http.Client{Timeout: timeout}, nil
444		}
445	
446		transport, ok := http.DefaultTransport.(*http.Transport)
447		if !ok {
448			return nil, fmt.Errorf("http.DefaultTransport is not *http.Transport")
449		}
450		transport = transport.Clone()
451		transport.TLSClientConfig = &tls.Config{
452			MinVersion:         tls.VersionTLS12,
453			InsecureSkipVerify: insecureSkipVerify, //nolint:gosec // intentional; documented as testing-only
454		}
455	
456		if caData != nil {
457			pool, err := x509.SystemCertPool()
458			if err != nil {
459				pool = x509.NewCertPool()
460			}
461			if !pool.AppendCertsFromPEM(caData) {
462				return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to append CA certificate to pool")
463			}
464			transport.TLSClientConfig.RootCAs = pool
465		}
466	
467		return &http.Client{
468			Timeout:   timeout,
469			Transport: transport,
470		}, nil
471	}

473	// fetchHTTP performs a GET to req.URL.
474	// When req.ETag is set it is sent as If-None-Match; when req.LastModified is set (and ETag is empty)
475	// it is sent as If-Modified-Since. A 304 response is returned as Result{Unchanged: true}.
476	// If req.VerifyChecksum is true, fetches req.URL+".sha256" and verifies integrity.
477	func fetchHTTP(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, req Request) (Result, error) {
478		var extraHeaders map[string]string
479		switch {
480		case req.ETag != "":
481			extraHeaders = map[string]string{headerIfNoneMatch: req.ETag}
482		case req.LastModified != "":
483			extraHeaders = map[string]string{headerIfModifiedSince: req.LastModified}
484		}
485	
486		data, respHeaders, statusCode, err := doGetWithHeaders(ctx, client, req.URL, req.Auth, extraHeaders,
487			http.StatusOK, http.StatusNotModified)
488		if err != nil {
489			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch bundle: %w", err)
490		}
491	
492		if statusCode == http.StatusNotModified {
493			// Return any updated validators from the 304 response so the caller can persist a
494			// rotated ETag or Last-Modified without treating the bundle as changed.
495			return Result{
496				Unchanged:    true,
497				ETag:         respHeaders.Get(headerETag),
498				LastModified: respHeaders.Get(headerLastModified),
499			}, nil
500		}
501	
502		actualChecksum := ComputeChecksum(data)
503	
504		if req.VerifyChecksum {
505			checksumURL := req.URL + ".sha256"
506			checksumData, _, _, err := doGetWithHeaders(ctx, client, checksumURL, req.Auth, nil, http.StatusOK)
507			if err != nil {
508				return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch bundle checksum from %s: %w", checksumURL, err)
509			}
510			fields := strings.Fields(string(checksumData))
511			if len(fields) == 0 {
512				return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("checksum file at %s is empty", checksumURL)
513			}
514			expectedChecksum := strings.ToLower(fields[0])
515			if _, err := hex.DecodeString(expectedChecksum); err != nil || len(expectedChecksum) != 64 {
516				return Result{}, fmt.Errorf(
517					"checksum file at %s contains invalid checksum %q: expected 64 hex characters",
518					checksumURL, fields[0],
519				)
520			}
521			if expectedChecksum != actualChecksum {
522				return Result{}, &nonTransientError{
523					err: fmt.Errorf("bundle checksum mismatch: expected %s, got %s", expectedChecksum, actualChecksum),
524				}
525			}
526		}
527	
528		return Result{
529			Data:         data,
530			Checksum:     actualChecksum,
531			ETag:         respHeaders.Get(headerETag),
532			LastModified: respHeaders.Get(headerLastModified),
533		}, nil
534	}

536	// nimBundleItem is a single entry in the NIM bundles API response.
537	type nimBundleItem struct {
538		Content  string `json:"content"`
539		Metadata struct {
540			Hash      string `json:"hash"`
541			Created   string `json:"created"`
542			PolicyUID string `json:"policyUID"`
543		} `json:"metadata"`
544	}

546	// nimResponse is the JSON envelope returned by the NIM bundles API.
547	type nimResponse struct {
548		Items []nimBundleItem `json:"items"`
549	}

551	// latestNIMItem returns the item with the most recent metadata.created timestamp.
552	// The NIM bundles API may return multiple compiled bundles for the same policy name
553	// (one per compilation) in an undefined order. This function selects the most recently
554	// compiled bundle by comparing RFC 3339 timestamps.
555	// Falls back to the last item if no timestamps are parseable.
556	func latestNIMItem(items []nimBundleItem) nimBundleItem {
557		best := len(items) - 1
558		var bestTime time.Time
559	
560		for i, item := range items {
561			t, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, item.Metadata.Created)
562			if err != nil {
563				continue
564			}
565			if t.After(bestTime) {
566				bestTime = t
567				best = i
568			}
569		}
570	
571		return items[best]
572	}

574	// fetchNIM calls the NIM security policies bundles API and decodes the bundle from the response.
575	//
576	// When a request targets a policy by name, and not by PolicyUID, NIM may return multiple compilations.
577	// This results in downloading the content multiple past compilations.
578	// To avoid this, a lightweight metadata-only request is issued first to identify the most recently
579	// compiled bundle via its created timestamp.
580	// This ensures a single bundle is fetched by its policyUID.
581	func fetchNIM(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, req Request, logger logr.Logger) (Result, error) {
582		policyUID := req.NIM.PolicyUID
583	
584		// When fetching by policyName, resolve to the latest compilation's policyUID first.
585		if policyUID == "" {
586			var err error
587			policyUID, err = resolveLatestNIMPolicyUID(ctx, client, req, logger)
588			if err != nil {
589				return Result{}, err
590			}
591		}
592	
593		logger.V(1).Info("Fetching NIM bundle by policyUID", "policyUID", policyUID)
594	
595		return fetchNIMByUID(ctx, client, req, policyUID)
596	}

598	// resolveLatestNIMPolicyUID performs a metadata-only NIM request to find all compilations for the
599	// given policy name, then returns the policyUID of the most recently compiled bundle.
600	func resolveLatestNIMPolicyUID(
601		ctx context.Context,
602		client *http.Client,
603		req Request,
604		logger logr.Logger,
605	) (string, error) {
606		metadataURL, err := buildNIMMetadataURL(req.URL, req.PolicyName, "")
607		if err != nil {
608			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to build NIM metadata URL: %w", err)
609		}
610	
611		body, err := doGet(ctx, client, metadataURL, req.Auth)
612		if err != nil {
613			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch NIM bundle metadata: %w", err)
614		}
615	
616		var resp nimResponse
617		if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &resp); err != nil {
618			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to parse NIM metadata response: %w", err)
619		}
620	
621		if len(resp.Items) == 0 {
622			return "", fmt.Errorf("NIM metadata response contains no items for policy %q", req.PolicyName)
623		}
624	
625		logger.V(1).Info(
626			"Resolved NIM policy compilations",
627			"policyName", req.PolicyName,
628			"compilationCount", len(resp.Items),
629		)
630	
631		latest := latestNIMItem(resp.Items)
632		if latest.Metadata.PolicyUID == "" {
633			return "", fmt.Errorf("NIM metadata response contains no policyUID for policy %q", req.PolicyName)
634		}
635	
636		logger.V(1).Info(
637			"Selected latest NIM compilation",
638			"policyName", req.PolicyName,
639			"policyUID", latest.Metadata.PolicyUID,
640			"created", latest.Metadata.Created,
641		)
642	
643		return latest.Metadata.PolicyUID, nil
644	}

646	// fetchNIMByUID fetches a single NIM bundle by its policyUID.
647	func fetchNIMByUID(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, req Request, policyUID string) (Result, error) {
648		nimURL, err := buildNIMURL(req.URL, "", policyUID)
649		if err != nil {
650			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to build NIM URL: %w", err)
651		}
652	
653		body, err := doGet(ctx, client, nimURL, req.Auth)
654		if err != nil {
655			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch bundle from NIM: %w", err)
656		}
657	
658		var resp nimResponse
659		if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &resp); err != nil {
660			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse NIM response: %w", err)
661		}
662	
663		if len(resp.Items) == 0 {
664			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("NIM response contains no items for policy UID %q", policyUID)
665		}
666	
667		// A policyUID-targeted request returns exactly one item.
668		item := resp.Items[0]
669	
670		data, err := base64.StdEncoding.DecodeString(item.Content)
671		if err != nil {
672			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to base64-decode NIM bundle content: %w", err)
673		}
674	
675		actualChecksum := ComputeChecksum(data)
676		bundleHash := item.Metadata.Hash
677	
678		// Verify the downloaded bundle against the hash reported by the NIM API,
679		// unless the caller supplied their own ExpectedChecksum via BundleValidation
680		// (the outer Fetch loop will check that instead).
681		if bundleHash != "" && req.ExpectedChecksum == "" {
682			if actualChecksum != strings.ToLower(bundleHash) {
683				return Result{}, &nonTransientError{
684					err: fmt.Errorf("NIM bundle integrity check failed: expected %s, got %s", bundleHash, actualChecksum),
685				}
686			}
687		}
688	
689		return Result{Data: data, Checksum: actualChecksum}, nil
690	}

705			fmt.Sprintf("/api/platform/v1/security/logprofiles/%s/%s/bundle", req.LogProfileName, versionResp.Version)
706		body, err = doGet(ctx, client, logProfileBundleURL, req.Auth)

753	// fetchN1C resolves the N1C policy name (and optional version) to internal object IDs via the
754	// N1C policies API, then downloads the compiled bundle from the compile endpoint.
755	// Authentication uses the APIToken scheme rather than Bearer.
756	//
757	// Flow:
758	//  1. GET /api/nginx/one/namespaces/<ns>/app-protect/policies — find policy by name, get pol_obj_id.
759	//     The response also includes latest.object_id, so no second call is needed when policyVersionID is empty.
760	//  2. If req.N1CPolicyVersionID is set, it is used directly as pol_version_id (no versions list call needed).
761	//     Otherwise the latest version ID from step 1 (or findN1CLatestVersion) is used.
762	//  3. GET .../policies/<pol_obj_id>/versions/<pol_version_id>/compile?nap_release=<release>
763	//     Poll until status is "succeeded" (or fail on "failed"). The response includes the bundle hash.
764	//  4. GET .../compile?nap_release=<release>&download=true — download the binary bundle.
765	//     Verify it against the hash returned in step 3.
766	func fetchN1C(
767		ctx context.Context,
768		client *http.Client,
769		req Request,
770		pollDelay time.Duration,
771		logger logr.Logger,
772	) (Result, error) {
773		polObjID, polVersionID, err := resolveN1CIDs(ctx, client, req)
774		if err != nil {
775			return Result{}, err
776		}
777	
778		statusURL, err := buildN1CCompileStatusURL(req.URL, req.N1C.Namespace, polObjID, polVersionID)
779		if err != nil {
780			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to build N1C compile status URL: %w", err)
781		}
782	
783		compileTimeout := defaultTimeout
784		if req.Timeout != nil {
785			compileTimeout = req.Timeout.Duration
786		}
787		pollCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, compileTimeout)
788		defer cancel()
789	
790		bundleHash, err := pollN1CCompileStatus(pollCtx, client, statusURL, req.Auth, pollDelay, logger, "policy")
791		if err != nil {
792			return Result{}, err
793		}
794	
795		downloadURL, err := buildN1CCompileDownloadURL(req.URL, req.N1C.Namespace, polObjID, polVersionID)
796		if err != nil {
797			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to build N1C compile download URL: %w", err)
798		}
799	
800		body, err := doGet(ctx, client, downloadURL, req.Auth)
801		if err != nil {
802			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch N1C compiled bundle: %w", err)
803		}
804	
805		actualChecksum := ComputeChecksum(body)
806	
807		// Verify the downloaded bundle against the hash reported by the N1C API,
808		// unless the caller supplied their own ExpectedChecksum via BundleValidation
809		// (the outer Fetch loop will check that instead).
810		if bundleHash != "" && req.ExpectedChecksum == "" {
811			if actualChecksum != strings.ToLower(bundleHash) {
812				return Result{}, &nonTransientError{
813					err: fmt.Errorf("N1C bundle integrity check failed: expected %s, got %s", bundleHash, actualChecksum),
814				}
815			}
816		}
817	
818		return Result{Data: body, Checksum: actualChecksum}, nil
819	}
820	
821	// pollN1CCompileStatus polls the N1C compile status endpoint until the compilation job succeeds or
822	// fails. It returns the bundle hash on success.
823	// Transient errors (5xx, network failures) are treated as "still pending" and retried after
824	// pollDelay. Non-transient errors (4xx) are returned immediately.
825	// kind is a short human-readable label used in log messages (e.g. "policy", "log profile").
826	func pollN1CCompileStatus(
827		ctx context.Context,
828		client *http.Client,
829		statusURL string,
830		auth *BundleAuth,
831		pollDelay time.Duration,
832		logger logr.Logger,
833		kind string,
834	) (string, error) {
835		for {
836			body, err := doGet(ctx, client, statusURL, auth, http.StatusOK, http.StatusAccepted)
837			if err != nil {
838				var nte *nonTransientError
839				if errors.As(err, &nte) {
840					return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to check N1C %s compile status: %w", kind, err)
841				}
842				// Transient error (5xx, network) — log and retry.
843				logger.V(1).Info("Transient error polling N1C compile status, retrying",
844					"kind", kind, "error", err)
845			} else {
846				var status n1cCompileStatusResponse
847				if parseErr := json.Unmarshal(body, &status); parseErr != nil {
848					return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to parse N1C %s compile status response: %w", kind, parseErr)
849				}
850	
851				switch status.Status {
852				case "succeeded":
853					logger.V(1).Info("N1C bundle compilation succeeded", "kind", kind, "hash", status.Hash)
854					return status.Hash, nil
855				case "failed":
856					return "", &nonTransientError{
857						err: fmt.Errorf("N1C %s bundle compilation failed", kind),
858					}
859				default:
860					logger.V(1).Info("N1C bundle compilation in progress", "kind", kind, "status", status.Status)
861				}
862				// Any other status (e.g. "pending") — fall through to wait and retry.
863			}
864	
865			select {
866			case <-ctx.Done():
867				return "", ctx.Err()
868			case <-time.After(pollDelay):
869			}
870		}
871	}
872	
873	// fetchN1CLogProfile resolves the log profile object ID via the N1C log profiles API (when needed),
874	// then downloads the compiled bundle from the compile endpoint.
875	// Authentication uses the APIToken scheme rather than Bearer.
876	//
877	// Flow:
878	//  1. If req.N1C.LogProfileObjectID is set, use it directly (skip the list call).
879	//     Otherwise, GET .../app-protect/log-profiles — find the profile by req.LogProfileName.
880	//  2. GET .../app-protect/log-profiles/<lp_obj_id>/compile?nap_release=<release>&download=true
881	func fetchN1CLogProfile(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, req Request) (Result, error) {
882		lpObjID := req.N1C.LogProfileObjectID
883		if lpObjID == "" {
884			var err error
885			lpObjID, err = findN1CLogProfile(ctx, client, req.URL, req.N1C.Namespace, req.LogProfileName, req.Auth)
886			if err != nil {
887				return Result{}, err
888			}
889		}
890	
891		compileURL, err := buildN1CLogProfileCompileURL(req.URL, req.N1C.Namespace, lpObjID)
892		if err != nil {
893			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to build N1C log profile compile URL: %w", err)
894		}
895	
896		body, err := doGet(ctx, client, compileURL, req.Auth)
897		if err != nil {
898			return Result{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch N1C compiled log profile bundle: %w", err)
899		}
900	
901		return Result{Data: body, Checksum: ComputeChecksum(body)}, nil
902	}

904	// n1cPagedResult is the common JSON envelope shape shared by the N1C list APIs.
905	// The generic parameter T is the item type in the page.
906	type n1cPagedResult[T any] struct {
907		Items []T `json:"items"`
908		Total int `json:"total"`
909	}
910	
911	// paginatedSearch iterates through pages of N1C list API results until pred returns true for an
912	// item or all pages are exhausted. buildURL receives the current (1-based) offset and page size
913	// and must return the URL to fetch. pred is called for each item; when it returns (result, true),
914	// iteration stops and result is returned. errMsg is used in error context strings.
915	func paginatedSearch[T any](
916		ctx context.Context,
917		client *http.Client,
918		auth *BundleAuth,
919		buildURL func(offset, limit int) (string, error),
920		pred func(item T) (result T, found bool),
921		errMsg string,
922	) (T, error) {
923		const pageSize = 100
924	
925		for offset := 1; ; offset += pageSize {
926			listURL, err := buildURL(offset, pageSize)
927			if err != nil {
928				var zero T
929				return zero, fmt.Errorf("failed to build %s URL: %w", errMsg, err)
930			}
931	
932			body, err := doGet(ctx, client, listURL, auth)
933			if err != nil {
934				var zero T
935				return zero, fmt.Errorf("failed to list %s: %w", errMsg, err)
936			}
937	
938			var resp n1cPagedResult[T]
939			if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &resp); err != nil {
940				var zero T
941				return zero, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse %s response: %w", errMsg, err)
942			}
943	
944			for _, item := range resp.Items {
945				if result, found := pred(item); found {
946					return result, nil
947				}
948			}
949	
950			if offset+pageSize-1 >= resp.Total {
951				break
952			}
953		}
954	
955		var zero T
956		return zero, fmt.Errorf("%s not found", errMsg)
957	}

1193	// fetchNIMChecksum fetches only the metadata for a NIM policy bundle and returns the hash
1194	// without downloading the bundle content.
1195	// When the response contains multiple compilations, the hash of the most recently compiled
1196	// bundle (by metadata.created timestamp) is returned.
1197	func fetchNIMChecksum(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, req Request, logger logr.Logger) (string, error) {
1198		nimURL, err := buildNIMMetadataURL(req.URL, req.PolicyName, req.NIM.PolicyUID)
1199		if err != nil {
1200			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to build NIM metadata URL: %w", err)
1201		}
1202	
1203		body, err := doGet(ctx, client, nimURL, req.Auth)
1204		if err != nil {
1205			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch NIM bundle metadata: %w", err)
1206		}
1207	
1208		var resp nimResponse
1209		if err := json.Unmarshal(body, &resp); err != nil {
1210			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to parse NIM metadata response: %w", err)
1211		}
1212	
1213		policyRef := req.PolicyName
1214		if policyRef == "" {
1215			policyRef = req.NIM.PolicyUID
1216		}
1217		if len(resp.Items) == 0 {
1218			return "", fmt.Errorf("NIM response contains no items for policy %q", policyRef)
1219		}
1220	
1221		latest := latestNIMItem(resp.Items)
1222		hash := strings.ToLower(latest.Metadata.Hash)
1223		if hash == "" {
1224			return "", fmt.Errorf("NIM response contains no hash for policy %q", policyRef)
1225		}
1226	
1227		logger.V(1).Info(
1228			"Fetched NIM policy checksum",
1229			"policyRef", policyRef,
1230			"policyUID", latest.Metadata.PolicyUID,
1231			"created", latest.Metadata.Created,
1232			"hash", hash,
1233		)
1234	
1235		return hash, nil
1236	}

1238	// fetchNIMLogProfileChecksum fetches the NIM log profile bundle and computes its SHA-256 checksum.
1239	// Unlike NIM policy bundles, NIM log profiles have no metadata-only endpoint, so the full bundle
1240	// must be downloaded and decoded to obtain the checksum.
1241	func fetchNIMLogProfileChecksum(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, req Request) (string, error) {
1242		compilerVersionURL := strings.TrimRight(req.URL, "/") + "/api/platform/v1/security/nap-compiler/versions/latest"
1243		body, err := doGet(ctx, client, compilerVersionURL, req.Auth)
1244		if err != nil {
1245			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch latest NIM NAP compiler version: %w", err)
1246		}
1247	
1248		var versionResp NAPCompilerVersionResponse
1249		if err = json.Unmarshal(body, &versionResp); err != nil {
1250			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to parse NIM NAP compiler version response: %w", err)
1251		}
1252	
1253		// The log profile bundle endpoint returns the full bundle including metadata; there is no
1254		// metadata-only endpoint in NIM. We fetch the full response but discard the bundle bytes,
1255		// using only the checksum computed from the decoded content.
1256		logProfileBundleURL := strings.TrimRight(req.URL, "/") +
1257			fmt.Sprintf("/api/platform/v1/security/logprofiles/%s/%s/bundle", req.LogProfileName, versionResp.Version)
1258		body, err = doGet(ctx, client, logProfileBundleURL, req.Auth)
1259		if err != nil {
1260			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to fetch NIM log profile bundle metadata: %w", err)
1261		}
1262	
1263		var resp LogProfileBundleResponse
1264		if err = json.Unmarshal(body, &resp); err != nil {
1265			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to parse NIM log profile bundle response: %w", err)
1266		}
1267	
1268		data, err := base64.StdEncoding.DecodeString(resp.CompiledBundle)
1269		if err != nil {
1270			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to base64-decode NIM log profile bundle content: %w", err)
1271		}
1272	
1273		return ComputeChecksum(data), nil
1274	}

1350	// buildNIMBundlesURL constructs the NIM bundles API URL. When includeBundleContent is true the
1351	// response includes the base64-encoded bundle; when false only metadata (including the hash) is returned.
1352	func buildNIMBundlesURL(baseURL, policyName, policyUID string, includeBundleContent bool) (string, error) {
1353		base, err := url.Parse(baseURL)
1354		if err != nil {
1355			return "", fmt.Errorf("invalid NIM base URL %q: %w", baseURL, err)
1356		}
1357		base.Path = strings.TrimRight(base.Path, "/") + "/api/platform/v1/security/policies/bundles"
1358		base.Fragment = ""
1359		q := url.Values{}
1360		if includeBundleContent {
1361			q.Set("includeBundleContent", "true")
1362		} else {
1363			q.Set("includeBundleContent", "false")
1364		}
1365		// NIM defaults startTime to now-24h when omitted, which silently excludes policies that
1366		// haven't been recompiled in the last 24 hours. Set it to the Unix epoch to return all
1367		// matching policies regardless of age.
1368		q.Set("startTime", unixEpochRFC3339)
1369		if policyUID != "" {
1370			q.Set("policyUID", policyUID)
1371		} else {
1372			q.Set("policyName", policyName)
1373		}
1374		base.RawQuery = q.Encode()

1384	//
1385	// NIM log profile bundles have no metadata-only endpoint and require a full download to compute a
1386	// checksum, so they return false.
1387	// Plain HTTP sources also always require a full download.
1388	func (r Request) SupportsChecksumOnlyFetch() bool {
1389		// NIM log-profile requests have no metadata-only endpoint regardless of other fields.
1390		if r.LogProfileName != "" && r.N1C.Namespace == "" {
1391			return false
1392		}
1393		return r.N1C.Namespace != "" || r.PolicyName != "" || r.NIM.PolicyUID != ""

1396	// doGet performs a GET request and returns the response body.
1397	// acceptedCodes lists the HTTP status codes treated as success; defaults to 200 OK when empty.
1398	func doGet(ctx context.Context, client *http.Client, rawURL string, auth *BundleAuth, acceptedCodes ...int,
1399	) ([]byte, error) {
1400		body, _, _, err := doGetWithHeaders(ctx, client, rawURL, auth, nil, acceptedCodes...)
1401		return body, err
1402	}
1403	
1404	// doGetWithHeaders performs a GET request with optional extra request headers and returns the
1405	// response body, response headers, status code, and any error.
1406	// acceptedCodes lists the HTTP status codes treated as success; defaults to 200 OK when empty.
1407	// For accepted non-200 codes (e.g. 304) the body will be empty and no error is returned.
1408	func doGetWithHeaders(
1409		ctx context.Context,
1410		client *http.Client,
1411		rawURL string,
1412		auth *BundleAuth,
1413		extraHeaders map[string]string,
1414		acceptedCodes ...int,
1415	) ([]byte, http.Header, int, error) {
1416		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, rawURL, nil)
1417		if err != nil {
1418			return nil, nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("failed to create request for %s: %w", rawURL, err)
1419		}
1420	
1421		if auth != nil {
1422			switch {
1423			case auth.APIToken != "":
1424				req.Header.Set("Authorization", "APIToken "+auth.APIToken)
1425			case auth.BearerToken != "":
1426				req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+auth.BearerToken)
1427			case auth.Username != "":
1428				req.SetBasicAuth(auth.Username, auth.Password)
1429			}
1430		}
1431	
1432		for k, v := range extraHeaders {
1433			req.Header.Set(k, v)
1434		}
1435	
1436		resp, err := client.Do(req)
1437		if err != nil {
1438			return nil, nil, 0, fmt.Errorf("request to %s failed: %w", rawURL, err)
1439		}
1440		defer resp.Body.Close()
1441	
1442		accepted := acceptedCodes
1443		if len(accepted) == 0 {
1444			accepted = []int{http.StatusOK}
1445		}
1446		if !slices.Contains(accepted, resp.StatusCode) {
1447			err := fmt.Errorf("unexpected status %d from %s", resp.StatusCode, rawURL)
1448			if resp.StatusCode >= http.StatusBadRequest && resp.StatusCode < http.StatusInternalServerError {
1449				return nil, nil, resp.StatusCode, &nonTransientError{err: err}
1450			}
1451			return nil, nil, resp.StatusCode, err
1452		}
1453	
1454		// 304 has no body; return early with just the status.
1455		if resp.StatusCode == http.StatusNotModified {
1456			return nil, resp.Header, resp.StatusCode, nil
1457		}
1458	
1459		data, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
1460		if err != nil {
1461			return nil, nil, resp.StatusCode, fmt.Errorf("failed to read response from %s: %w", rawURL, err)
1462		}
1463		return data, resp.Header, resp.StatusCode, nil
1464	}
1465	
1466	// ComputeChecksum returns the lowercase hex-encoded SHA-256 of data.
1467	func ComputeChecksum(data []byte) string {
1468		sum := sha256.Sum256(data)
1469		return hex.EncodeToString(sum[:])
1470	}

140	// ReconcilePoller ensures a poller is running with the correct configuration.
141	// If no poller exists, one is started. If a poller exists with the same sources,
142	// only the target deployments are updated. If sources have changed, the poller is restarted.
143	// This avoids unnecessary poller restarts when only targets change.
144	func (m *pollerManager) ReconcilePoller(ctx context.Context, cfg Config) {
145		if len(cfg.Sources) == 0 {
146			m.logger.V(1).Info("No polling sources, not starting poller", "policy", cfg.PolicyNsName)
147			return
148		}
149	
150		m.mu.Lock()
151		entry, exists := m.pollers[cfg.PolicyNsName]
152	
153		// If poller exists and sources haven't changed, just update targets if needed.
154		if exists && sourcesEqual(entry.poller.getSources(), cfg.Sources) {
155			m.mu.Unlock()
156			entry.poller.updateTargetDeployments(cfg.TargetDeployments)
157			return
158		}
159		m.mu.Unlock()
160	
161		// Sources changed or poller doesn't exist - need to (re)start.
162		m.startPoller(ctx, cfg)
163	}

165	// startPoller starts a new poller, stopping any existing one first.
166	func (m *pollerManager) startPoller(ctx context.Context, cfg Config) {
167		m.mu.Lock()
168		defer m.mu.Unlock()
169	
170		// Always cancel any existing poller before overwriting the map entry.
171		// This is safe even if the caller didn't observe one, because another
172		// goroutine may have started one between our check and acquiring this lock.
173		if entry, exists := m.pollers[cfg.PolicyNsName]; exists {
174			m.logger.V(1).Info("Stopping existing poller before starting new one", "policy", cfg.PolicyNsName)
175			entry.cancel()
176			delete(m.pollErrors, cfg.PolicyNsName)
177			delete(m.bundleUpdates, cfg.PolicyNsName)
178			m.clearBundleCacheLocked(entry.poller)
179		}
180	
181		pollerCtx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx)
182	
183		var poller *poller
184	
185		// Create a wrapped callback that records poll results and triggers a status update
186		// scoped to just the poller's target deployments.
187		wrappedCallback := func(
188			policyNsName types.NamespacedName,
189			bundleKey graph.WAFBundleKey,
190			newChecksum string,
191			err error,
192		) {
193			m.mu.RLock()
194			desc := m.bundleKeyToDescription[bundleKey]
195			m.mu.RUnlock()
196			if desc == "" {
197				desc = "WAF bundle"
198			}
199			m.recordPollResult(policyNsName, bundleKey, desc, newChecksum, err)
200			if m.statusCallback != nil {
201				m.statusCallback(poller.getTargetDeployments())
202			}
203		}
204	
205		// Record which policy owns each bundle key and its human-readable description.
206		for _, src := range cfg.Sources {
207			m.bundleKeyToPolicy[src.BundleKey] = cfg.PolicyNsName
208			desc := src.Description
209			if desc == "" {
210				desc = "WAF bundle"
211			}
212			m.bundleKeyToDescription[src.BundleKey] = desc
213		}
214	
215		poller = newPoller(pollerConfig{
216			logger:               m.logger,
217			policyNsName:         cfg.PolicyNsName,
218			sources:              cfg.Sources,
219			fetcher:              m.fetcher,
220			deployments:          m.deployments,
221			targetDeployments:    cfg.TargetDeployments,
222			initialChecksums:     cfg.InitialChecksums,
223			statusCallback:       wrappedCallback,
224			bundleUpdateCallback: m.cacheBundleUpdate,
225		})
226	
227		m.pollers[cfg.PolicyNsName] = &pollerEntry{
228			poller: poller,
229			cancel: cancel,
230		}
231	
232		go func() {
233			poller.run(pollerCtx)
234			// Clean up after poller exits.
235			m.mu.Lock()
236			// Only delete if this is still the same poller (could have been replaced).
237			if entry, exists := m.pollers[cfg.PolicyNsName]; exists && entry.poller == poller {
238				delete(m.pollers, cfg.PolicyNsName)
239			}
240			m.mu.Unlock()
241		}()
242	
243		m.logger.Info("Started WAF poller", "policy", cfg.PolicyNsName, "sourceCount", len(cfg.Sources))
244	}

246	// recordPollResult records the result of a poll attempt for a policy.
247	// If err is nil, it clears any previous error for the same bundle key.
248	// If newChecksum is non-empty, the bundle was successfully updated and the update is recorded.
249	// If err is non-nil, it stores the error for this bundle key.
250	// This method is called by the internal status callback.
251	func (m *pollerManager) recordPollResult(
252		policyNsName types.NamespacedName,
253		bundleKey graph.WAFBundleKey,
254		bundleDescription string,
255		newChecksum string,
256		err error,
257	) {
258		m.mu.Lock()
259		defer m.mu.Unlock()
260	
261		if err == nil {
262			if existing := m.pollErrors[policyNsName]; existing != nil && existing.BundleKey == bundleKey {
263				delete(m.pollErrors, policyNsName)
264			}
265			if newChecksum != "" {
266				m.bundleUpdates[policyNsName] = BundleUpdate{
267					BundleKey:         bundleKey,
268					BundleDescription: bundleDescription,
269					Checksum:          newChecksum,
270					UpdatedAt:         metav1.Now(),
271				}
272			}
273		} else {
274			m.pollErrors[policyNsName] = &PollError{
275				BundleKey:         bundleKey,
276				BundleDescription: bundleDescription,
277				Err:               err,
278			}
279		}
280	}

282	// GetAllBundleUpdates returns a copy of the most recent successful bundle update per policy.
283	func (m *pollerManager) GetAllBundleUpdates() map[types.NamespacedName]BundleUpdate {
284		m.mu.RLock()
285		defer m.mu.RUnlock()
286	
287		if len(m.bundleUpdates) == 0 {
288			return nil
289		}
290	
291		result := make(map[types.NamespacedName]BundleUpdate, len(m.bundleUpdates))
292		maps.Copy(result, m.bundleUpdates)
293		return result
294	}

296	// cacheBundleUpdate stores the latest successfully polled bundle data in the manager's cache.
297	// This is called by pollers when they detect a changed bundle, ensuring the freshest data
298	// is available for graph rebuild stale-bundle fallback.
299	// On the first time a bundle key appears in this manager's cache, a WAFBundleReconcileEvent
300	// is injected into the event loop to trigger an immediate graph rebuild.
301	// Note: this fires on any first-cache event, including after a poller restart that cleared the
302	// cache — not only when the policy was previously in BundlePending state. A spurious reconcile
303	// event in that case is harmless: it triggers an unnecessary graph rebuild but causes no
304	// incorrect behavior.
305	func (m *pollerManager) cacheBundleUpdate(bundleKey graph.WAFBundleKey, data []byte, checksum string) {
306		m.mu.Lock()
307	
308		_, alreadyCached := m.bundleCache[bundleKey]
309	
310		m.bundleCache[bundleKey] = &graph.WAFBundleData{
311			Data:     data,
312			Checksum: checksum,
313		}
314	
315		// Capture event details while holding the lock, then release before sending.
316		var event *events.WAFBundleReconcileEvent
317		if !alreadyCached {
318			if policyNsName, ok := m.bundleKeyToPolicy[bundleKey]; ok && m.eventCh != nil {
319				event = &events.WAFBundleReconcileEvent{PolicyNsName: policyNsName}
320			}
321		}
322	
323		m.mu.Unlock()
324	
325		// Send the reconcile event after releasing the lock so other manager operations are not
326		// blocked on the mutex while waiting for the event loop. The manager's root context is
327		// used as a cancellation escape hatch: on shutdown, the event loop exits before the
328		// manager's context is canceled, so without this the poller goroutine could block
329		// indefinitely trying to send to an already-drained channel.
330		if event != nil {
331			select {
332			case m.eventCh <- *event:
333			case <-m.ctx.Done():
334			}
335		}
336	}

338	// GetAllPollErrors returns a deep copy of all current poll errors.
339	func (m *pollerManager) GetAllPollErrors() map[types.NamespacedName]PollError {
340		m.mu.RLock()
341		defer m.mu.RUnlock()
342	
343		if len(m.pollErrors) == 0 {
344			return nil
345		}
346	
347		result := make(map[types.NamespacedName]PollError, len(m.pollErrors))
348		for k, v := range m.pollErrors {
349			result[k] = *v
350		}
351		return result
352	}

354	// GetLatestBundles returns a copy of all bundles that have been successfully fetched by pollers.
355	// These represent the freshest known bundle data and should take precedence over
356	// graph-cached bundles when constructing stale-bundle fallback state.
357	func (m *pollerManager) GetLatestBundles() map[graph.WAFBundleKey]*graph.WAFBundleData {
358		m.mu.RLock()
359		defer m.mu.RUnlock()
360	
361		if len(m.bundleCache) == 0 {
362			return nil
363		}
364	
365		result := make(map[graph.WAFBundleKey]*graph.WAFBundleData, len(m.bundleCache))
366		maps.Copy(result, m.bundleCache)
367		return result
368	}

370	// StopPoller stops the poller for a WAFPolicy.
371	func (m *pollerManager) StopPoller(policyNsName types.NamespacedName) {
372		m.mu.Lock()
373		entry, exists := m.pollers[policyNsName]
374		if !exists {
375			m.mu.Unlock()
376			return
377		}
378		delete(m.pollers, policyNsName)
379		delete(m.pollErrors, policyNsName)
380		delete(m.bundleUpdates, policyNsName)
381		m.clearBundleCacheLocked(entry.poller)
382		m.mu.Unlock()
383	
384		entry.cancel()
385		m.logger.Info("Stopped WAF poller", "policy", policyNsName)
386	}
387	
388	// stopAll stops all running pollers. Should be called during shutdown.
389	func (m *pollerManager) stopAll() {
390		m.mu.Lock()
391		entries := make([]*pollerEntry, 0, len(m.pollers))
392		for _, entry := range m.pollers {
393			entries = append(entries, entry)
394		}
395		m.pollers = make(map[types.NamespacedName]*pollerEntry)
396		m.pollErrors = make(map[types.NamespacedName]*PollError)
397		m.bundleUpdates = make(map[types.NamespacedName]BundleUpdate)
398		m.bundleCache = make(map[graph.WAFBundleKey]*graph.WAFBundleData)
399		m.bundleKeyToPolicy = make(map[graph.WAFBundleKey]types.NamespacedName)
400		m.bundleKeyToDescription = make(map[graph.WAFBundleKey]string)
401		m.mu.Unlock()
402	
403		for _, entry := range entries {
404			entry.cancel()
405		}
406	
407		m.logger.Info("Stopped all WAF pollers", "count", len(entries))
408	}
409	
410	// clearBundleCacheLocked removes cached bundle data and all per-key mappings for all bundle keys
411	// owned by the given poller. Must be called while m.mu is held.
412	func (m *pollerManager) clearBundleCacheLocked(p *poller) {
413		for _, src := range p.getSources() {
414			delete(m.bundleCache, src.BundleKey)
415			delete(m.bundleKeyToPolicy, src.BundleKey)
416			delete(m.bundleKeyToDescription, src.BundleKey)
417		}
418	}
419	
420	// HasPoller reports whether a poller is currently registered for the given policy.
421	func (m *pollerManager) HasPoller(policyNsName types.NamespacedName) bool {
422		m.mu.RLock()
423		defer m.mu.RUnlock()
424		_, exists := m.pollers[policyNsName]
425		return exists
426	}
427	
428	// StopPollersNotIn stops all pollers whose policy namespace/name is not in the given set.
429	// This is used to clean up pollers for policies that have been deleted or no longer need polling.
430	func (m *pollerManager) StopPollersNotIn(activePolicies map[types.NamespacedName]struct{}) {
431		m.mu.Lock()
432		var toStop []types.NamespacedName
433		for nsName := range m.pollers {
434			if _, active := activePolicies[nsName]; !active {
435				toStop = append(toStop, nsName)
436			}
437		}
438	
439		entriesToCancel := make([]*pollerEntry, 0, len(toStop))
440		for _, nsName := range toStop {
441			if entry, exists := m.pollers[nsName]; exists {
442				entriesToCancel = append(entriesToCancel, entry)
443				delete(m.pollers, nsName)
444				delete(m.pollErrors, nsName)
445				delete(m.bundleUpdates, nsName)
446				m.clearBundleCacheLocked(entry.poller)
447			}
448		}
449		m.mu.Unlock()
450	
451		for _, entry := range entriesToCancel {
452			entry.cancel()
453		}
454	
455		if len(toStop) > 0 {
456			m.logger.Info("Stopped stale WAF pollers", "count", len(toStop))
457		}
458	}

31	// BundleSource represents a single bundle source that needs polling.
